documentation.readme: This program shows you IMSI numbers, country, brand and operator of cellphones around you.
usage_sections: USB DVB-T key (RTL2832U) with antenna (less than 15$)

arguments.name: -a
arguments.description: Show TMSI who haven't got IMSI (default : false)

arguments.description: Show TMSI who haven't got IMSI (default : false)

arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --mysql
arguments.description: Save observed IMSI values to specified MYSQL DB (copy

arguments.description: sniff on interface instead of listening on port

arguments.description: sniff on interface instead of listening on port

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: Save observed IMSI values to specified TXT file

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the ble category. Fits when the engagement needs: This program shows you IMSI numbers, country, brand and operator of cellphones around you.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: This program shows you IMSI numbers, country, brand and operator of cellphones around you.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/Oros42/IMSI-catcher.git; apt install python3-pip. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL && unzip -q master.zip`. Further invocations from upstream: `docker run -ti --net=host -e DISPLAY=$DISPLAY --privileged -v /dev/bus/usb:/dev/bus/usb  atomicpowerman/imsi-catcher bash`; `python3 simple_IMSI-catcher.py -h`. This program shows you IMSI numbers, country, brand and operator of cellphones around you.

name: IMSI-catcher
summary: This program shows you IMSI numbers, country, brand and operator of cellphones around you.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need ble, use IMSI-catcher because This program shows you IMSI numbers, country, brand and operator of cellphones around you.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `wget $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL && unzip -q master.zip` as documented upstream for IMSI-catcher.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `docker run -ti --net=host -e DISPLAY=$DISPLAY --privileged -v /dev/bus/usb:/dev/bus/usb  atomicpowerman/imsi-catcher bash` as documented upstream for IMSI-catcher.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 simple_IMSI-catcher.py -h` as documented upstream for IMSI-catcher.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Usage: simple_IMSI-catcher.py: [options]` as documented upstream for IMSI-catcher.
When chaining ble tools, pair IMSI-catcher with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `IMSI-catcher --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
